title: Luciola pilosa
taxonomy: Luciola pilosa is classified within the family Lampyidae, commonly known as fireflies or lightning bugs. This classification places it within the order Coleoptera, which consists of beetles. Within the family Lampyidae, Luciola pilosa is further grouped into the subfamily Luciolinae. The subfamily Luciolinae consists of fireflies that are often found in Asia and are known for their bioluminescent abilities. The species is also known as the hairy firefly due to its distinctive hairy body.
properties: Luciola pilosa is known for its therapeutic properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in treating various health conditions. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems due to its ability to reduce inflammation and combat infections. Additionally, Luciola pilosa is believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. More research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Luciola pilosa.
preparations: I don't have specific information on herbal preparations of Luciola pilosa.
